headline: Sellers Hold the Field as Geopolitical Fog Thickens Heading Into a Pivotal Week
body_html:
Friday's session closed the week on a defensive note, with decliners outpacing advancers by more than two to one — 326 names lower against just 147 higher across a 476-stock universe. Seven of eight tracked sectors finished in the red, led lower by Homebuilders, which shed more than three percent, and Healthcare and Industrials, each off roughly 1.1 to 1.2 percent. Energy stood as the lone bright spot, eking out a 0.77 percent gain, a move that reflected the market's uneasy eye on an escalating Middle East conflict.

The sharpest single-stock pain arrived in the medical-device space, where Intuitive Surgical tumbled 14.15 percent, dragging Healthcare lower alongside STAAR Surgical's 8.76 percent drop. Electronic-design automation software was equally punishing: Cadence Design fell 9.47 percent and Synopsys shed 7.85 percent, pressuring the Software sector. On the other side of the ledger, Sonos surged 5.89 percent and Seagate Technology climbed 5.66 percent, offering pockets of resilience in consumer electronics and storage.

The week ahead inherits considerable macro and geopolitical baggage. U.S. forces completed a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran as the calendar turned to Saturday, keeping energy markets and risk sentiment on edge. Options markets are flagging the possibility of outsized moves around upcoming mega-cap technology earnings, a dynamic that could reset the tape's tone well before Friday's close.

briefs.headline: Seventh Straight Night of U.S. Strikes on Iran Rattles Risk Appetite
briefs.body: American forces completed yet another round of attacks on Iran overnight Friday into Saturday, marking seven consecutive nights of military action. The sustained campaign deepened uncertainty in energy and shipping corridors, contributing to Friday's defensive posture across most sectors and keeping crude-linked names among the few areas of positive price action.

briefs.headline: EDA Software Pair Suffers Outsized Losses in Friday Trade
briefs.body: Cadence Design and Synopsys, two pillars of the electronic-design automation niche, fell 9.47 percent and 7.85 percent respectively on Friday, dragging the broader Software sector down nearly a full percent. The paired decline suggests company-specific or sector-level news hit both names simultaneously, a development traders will be scrutinising heading into the new week.

briefs.headline: Homebuilders Bear the Brunt as Rate-Sensitive Trades Unwind
briefs.body: The Homebuilders sector dropped 3.05 percent on Friday, the steepest sectoral decline in the universe and nearly triple the next-worst group. The move underscores lingering sensitivity to borrowing-cost expectations, and any commentary from Fed officials or fresh housing data in the coming week could amplify or reverse the slide.

briefs.headline: Gulf Tanker Seizure Adds a New Pressure Point for Shipping Markets
briefs.body: Suspected pirates seized a tanker off the Yemeni coast in the Gulf of Aden on Friday, sources reported, injecting fresh concern into an already strained maritime corridor. Coming alongside renewed Houthi-linked activity and the broader Iran conflict, the incident signals that seaborne supply-chain risk remains elevated entering the new week.

briefs.headline: Boeing Wins Back Airworthiness Sign-Off Authority as Demand Outlook Holds
briefs.body: Regulators at the FAA restored Boeing's ability to self-certify airworthiness on the 737 Max and 787 programmes, a meaningful step in the manufacturer's rehabilitation effort. Separately, Boeing kept its twenty-year jet-demand forecast unchanged despite the Iran conflict, projecting confidence that long-run commercial aviation fundamentals remain intact.

briefs.headline: Volatility Gauge Flags Potential for Big Swings Around Mega-Cap Earnings
briefs.body: A lesser-watched options-market volatility measure is signalling that investors are bracing for significant price moves tied to upcoming reports from the market's largest technology companies. With the earnings calendar set to heat up in the week ahead, the signal suggests hedging activity has picked up materially, a dynamic that could inject sharp intraday swings into an already unsettled tape.
